src: τῷ δὲ θανεῖν μὴ δυναμένῳ πνεύματι
tok: pres mp ptcp dat sg n
lit: For the spirit unable to die,
sm: for the spirit unable to die,

syn.mc: Preposed dative phrase completing the sentence begun on the next line.
syn.sub: No finite clause in this line.
syn.svo: No finite verb; the phrase is dative and participial, dependent on line 492.
syn.ptc: θανεῖν and δυναμένῳ characterize the spirit as incapable of death.
syn.rhet: The contrast between mortal death and immortal spirit frames the victory that follows.

lit: he killed the murderous death.
sm: he killed death, the destroyer of humans.

syn.mc: Main clause completing the thought of line 491.
syn.svo: Subject implied by context. Verb: ἀπέκτεινεν. Object: τὸν ἀνθρωποκτόνον θάνατον.
syn.rhet: Death itself is personified and paradoxically slain.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: For he himself, having been led like a lamb,
sm: for he himself, led like a lamb,

syn.mc: Participial clause introducing the sacrificial imagery.
syn.sub: No finite clause in this line; the participle is completed by line 494.
syn.svo: Subject: αὐτός. Participial verb: ἀχθείς. Comparative phrase: ὡς ἀμνός.
syn.ptc: ἀχθείς is circumstantial and anticipates the slaughter image of the next line.
syn.rhet: The exodus victory is reframed through sacrificial meekness.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: and as a sheep having been slain,
sm: and as a sheep slain,

syn.mc: Coordinate participial clause continuing line 493.
syn.svo: Subject remains implied he. Participial verb: σφαγείς. Comparative phrase: ὡς πρόβατον.
syn.ptc: σφαγείς reinforces the sacrificial parallel to the lamb image.
syn.rhet: The pair of participles intensifies the passion typology.

src: ἐλυτρώσατο ἡμᾶς ἐκ τῆς τοῦ κόσμου λατρείας
lit: He redeemed us from the service of the world,
sm: he redeemed us from the world's service,

syn.mc: Main clause.
syn.svo: Subject implied by context. Verb: ἐλυτρώσατο. Object: ἡμᾶς. Prepositional phrase: ἐκ τῆς τοῦ κόσμου λατρείας.
syn.rhet: Redemption is cast in exodus language.

lit: as from the land of Egypt,
sm: as from the land of Egypt,

syn.mc: Comparative prepositional phrase modifying line 495.
syn.svo: No finite verb; the phrase completes the exodus comparison.
syn.rhet: The redemption is explicitly aligned with the exodus.

src: καὶ ἔλυσεν ἐκ τῆς τοῦ διαβόλου δουλείας
lit: and he freed [us] from the slavery of the devil,
sm: and he freed us from the devil's slavery,

syn.mc: Coordinate main clause continuing the redemption sequence.
syn.svo: Subject implied by context. Verb: ἔλυσεν. Object implied: ἡμᾶς / us. Prepositional phrase: ἐκ τῆς τοῦ διαβόλου δουλείας.
syn.rhet: The slavery image mirrors the Egyptian bondage just named.

lit: as from Pharaoh's hand,
sm: as from Pharaoh's hand,

syn.mc: Comparative phrase modifying line 498.
syn.svo: No finite verb; this phrase completes the exodus parallel.
syn.rhet: The devil is implicitly equated with Pharaoh.

src: καὶ ἐσφράγισεν ἡμῶν τὰς ψυχὰς τῷ ἰδίῳ πνεύματι
lit: and he sealed our souls with his own spirit
sm: and he sealed our souls with his own spirit

syn.mc: Main clause.
syn.svo: Subject implied by context. Verb: ἐσφράγισεν. Object: τὰς ψυχὰς. Genitive of possession: ἡμῶν. Dative of means: τῷ ἰδίῳ πνεύματι.
syn.rhet: The seal image intensifies protection and ownership.

src: καὶ τὰ μέλη τοῦ σώματος τῷ ἰδίῳ αἵματι.
lit: and the members of the body with his own blood.
sm: and the members of the body with his own blood.

syn.mc: Coordinate phrase continuing the sealing clause from line 500.
syn.svo: Verb ἐσφράγισεν is carried over from line 500. Object: τὰ μέλη τοῦ σώματος. Dative of means: τῷ ἰδίῳ αἵματι.
syn.rhet: Spirit and blood form a paired sacramental safeguard.

src: οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ τὸν θάνατον ἐνδύσας αἰσχύνην
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: This is the one who clothed death with shame
sm: This is the one who clothed death with shame

syn.mc: Copular identification with an articular participle.
syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Participial clause: ὁ ... ἐνδύσας. Object of participle: τὸν θάνατον. Complement: αἰσχύνην.
syn.rhet: Death is personified and dishonored.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: and having set the devil mourning
sm: and making the devil mourn

syn.mc: Participial expansion parallel to line 502.
syn.svo: Participial subject: implied he. Verb: στήσας. Object: τὸν διάβολον. Complement: πενθήρη.
syn.rhet: The devil is not only defeated but made to grieve.

lit: as Moses [did to] Pharaoh.
sm: as Moses [did] to Pharaoh.

syn.mc: Comparative phrase completing the Moses/Pharaoh parallel.
syn.svo: Elliptical comparison with the verb omitted; the object is τὸν Φαραώ.
syn.rhet: The passion narrative is cast in the mold of Moses' victory over Pharaoh.

src: οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ τὴν ἀνομίαν πατάξας
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: This is the one who struck lawlessness
sm: This is the one who struck lawlessness

syn.mc: Copular identification with a participial descriptor.
syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Participial clause: ὁ ... πατάξας. Object: τὴν ἀνομίαν.
syn.rhet: Lawlessness is figured as an enemy dealt a blow.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: and having made injustice childless
sm: and made injustice childless

syn.mc: Coordinate participial expansion.
syn.svo: Participial subject implied by line 505. Verb: ἀτεκνώσας. Object: τὴν ἀδικίαν.
syn.rhet: Injustice is personified and rendered sterile.

lit: as Moses [did] Egypt.
sm: as Moses [did] Egypt.

syn.mc: Comparative phrase closing the parallel with line 505-506.
syn.svo: Elliptical comparison with the verb omitted; object: Αἴγυπτον.
syn.rhet: The devil's defeat is aligned with the overthrow of Egypt.

src: οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ ῥυσάμενος ἡμᾶς ἐκ δουλείας εἰς ἐλευθερίαν,
tok: aor mid ptcp nom sg m
lit: This is the one who rescued us from slavery into freedom,
sm: This is the one who rescued us from slavery into freedom,

syn.mc: Copular identification with a participial relative.
syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Participial clause: ὁ ῥυσάμενος. Object: ἡμᾶς. Source/goal phrases: ἐκ δουλείας / εἰς ἐλευθερίαν.
syn.rhet: The exodus pattern is made explicit in salvation language.

lit: out of darkness into light,
sm: out of darkness into light,

syn.mc: Prepositional parallelism continuing line 508.
syn.svo: No finite verb; the phrase depends on the participial rescue clause.
syn.rhet: Darkness and light form the first cosmic reversal.

lit: out of death into life,
sm: out of death into life,

syn.mc: Prepositional parallelism continuing line 508.
syn.svo: No finite verb; the phrase depends on the participial rescue clause.
syn.rhet: Death/life intensifies the rescue reversal.

lit: out of tyranny into an eternal kingdom,
sm: out of tyranny into an eternal kingdom,

syn.mc: Prepositional parallelism continuing line 508.
syn.svo: No finite verb; the phrase depends on the participial rescue clause.
syn.rhet: The final reversal moves from oppression to everlasting rule.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: and having made us a new priesthood
sm: and having made us a new priesthood

syn.mc: Participial continuation of line 508.
syn.svo: Participial subject implied by line 508. Verb: ποιήσας. Object: ἡμᾶς. Complement: ἱεράτευμα καινόν.
syn.rhet: The redeemed community is recast in cultic terms.

lit: and an everlasting chosen people.
sm: and an everlasting chosen people.
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a period.

syn.mc: Nominal complement continuing the participial clause begun in line 512.
syn.svo: Verb ἐποίησεν is carried over from line 512. Object/complement: λαὸν περιούσιον αἰώνιον.
syn.rhet: The covenant identity culminates in priesthood and peoplehood.

src: οὗτός ἐστιν τὸ πάσχα τῆς σωτηρίας ἡμῶν.
lit: This is the Passover of our salvation.
sm: This is the Passover of our salvation.

syn.mc: Simple copular identification.
syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Predicate nominative: τὸ πάσχα. Genitive complement: τῆς σωτηρίας ἡμῶν.
syn.rhet: The typology is named directly and climactically.

src: οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ ἐν πολλοῖς πολλὰ ὑπομείνας"
ana: οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ ἐν πολλοῖς πολλὰ ὑπομείνας.
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: This is the one who in many things endured many things.
sm: This is the one who endured many things in many places.

syn.mc: Copular identification with a participial descriptor.
syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Articular participle: ὁ ... ὑπομείνας. Prepositional phrase: ἐν πολλοῖς. Object of endurance: πολλὰ.
syn.rhet: The clause broadens the passion to a many-sided endurance.
syn.emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a period.

src: οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ ἐν τῷ Ἀβὲλ φονευθείς,
tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: This is the one murdered in Abel,
sm: This is the one murdered in Abel,

syn.mc: Copular identification introducing the typological series.
syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Participial clause: ὁ ἐν τῷ Ἀβὲλ φονευθείς.
syn.rhet: Abel becomes the first scriptural type of the slain one.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: and bound in Isaac,
sm: and bound in Isaac,

syn.mc: Coordinate participial item in the typological series.
syn.svo: Participial subject implied by the series. Verb: δεθείς. Locative/typological phrase: ἐν τῷ Ἰσὰκ.
syn.rhet: Isaac recalls the binding on Moriah.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: and sojourned in Jacob,
sm: and sojourned in Jacob,

syn.mc: Coordinate participial item in the typological series.
syn.svo: Participial subject implied by the series. Verb: ξενιτεύσας. Locative/typological phrase: ἐν τῷ Ἰακώβ.
syn.rhet: Jacob signifies the stranger's pilgrimage.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: and sold in Joseph,
sm: and sold in Joseph,

syn.mc: Coordinate participial item in the typological series.
syn.svo: Participial subject implied by the series. Verb: πραθείς. Locative/typological phrase: ἐν τῷ Ἰωσήφ.
syn.rhet: Joseph recalls betrayal and sale.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: and exposed in Moses,
sm: and exposed in Moses,

syn.mc: Final participial item in the batch, continuing beyond this file.
syn.svo: Participial subject implied by the series. Verb: ἐκτεθείς. Locative/typological phrase: ἐν τῷ Μωυσῇ.
syn.rhet: Moses recalls the infant exposure narrative and prepares the next items beyond the batch.
